name: Love Language
description: Discover how you give and receive love. Based on the five love languages framework, find out what truly makes you feel valued.

words.title: Words of Affirmation
words.description: Verbal expressions of love mean the world to you. A sincere compliment, a heartfelt message or a simple "I'm proud of you" can lift your spirits for days. You value open communication and expressing feelings out loud.
characteristics: Energised by kind words and verbal appreciation
Communicates affection through compliments
Remembers meaningful things people say
Hurt by harsh criticism or silence
Values honest, open conversations
words.strengths: You create an atmosphere where people feel truly seen and appreciated. Your affirmations strengthen every relationship you're in.
words.challenges: When others express love through actions rather than words, you might miss it. Learning to recognise non-verbal love can deepen your connections.

acts.title: Acts of Service
acts.description: Actions speak louder than words for you. When someone does something thoughtful without being asked — cooking dinner, fixing something, handling a difficult errand — you feel deeply loved and cared for.
characteristics: Values thoughtful, practical help
Shows love by doing things for others
Notices effort and initiative
Frustrated by laziness or broken promises
Believes love is demonstrated, not just spoken
acts.strengths: You are incredibly reliable and supportive. The people in your life know they can count on you when it matters most.
acts.challenges: Be careful not to overextend yourself doing everything for others. It's okay to ask for and accept help in return.

gifts.title: Receiving Gifts
gifts.description: It's not about materialism — for you, a thoughtful gift is a visible symbol of love and effort. A gift says "I was thinking about you." The thought, the timing and the meaning behind it matter far more than the price tag.
characteristics: Values meaningful, thoughtful presents
Remembers gift-giving occasions
Feels loved by visible tokens of affection
Hurt by forgotten occasions or careless gifts
Treasures sentimental items
gifts.strengths: You are wonderfully thoughtful when choosing gifts for others. Your attention to detail makes people feel truly known and cherished.
gifts.challenges: Help others understand what makes you feel loved. Not everyone connects gift-giving with love, so clear communication matters.

time.title: Quality Time
time.description: Your love language is undivided attention. When someone puts away their phone, looks you in the eyes and is truly present — that's love to you. Shared experiences, focused conversations and simply being together fill your cup.
characteristics: Values full, undivided presence
Loves shared activities and experiences
Feels closest when given focused attention
Hurt by distractions and cancelled plans
Prioritises time with loved ones over material things
time.strengths: You build the deepest, most meaningful bonds because you invest real time and presence into your relationships.
time.challenges: Life gets busy. Learning to feel loved even during brief, quality moments — rather than needing long stretches of time — helps you stay connected.

touch.title: Physical Touch
touch.description: A hug, a pat on the back, sitting close — physical connection communicates safety, warmth and love to you in a way no words can match. You feel most loved when there is physical affection and closeness in your relationships.
characteristics: Expresses affection through physical contact
Comforted by hugs and physical closeness
Sensitive to the physical presence of loved ones
Affected by lack of physical contact
Uses touch to comfort and reassure others
touch.strengths: You create an immediate sense of warmth and safety around you. Your physical expressiveness makes others feel welcome and loved.
touch.challenges: Be mindful that not everyone shares this love language. Checking in with others about their comfort level shows respect and deepens trust.

questions.question: After a tough week, what would make you feel most loved?
options: Someone telling you how much they appreciate you
A friend coming over and helping you sort things out
Receiving a small but thoughtful surprise gift
A long catch-up with your favourite person, phones away
A warm hug that lasts longer than a second

questions.question: What upsets you most in a close relationship?
options: Not hearing enough appreciation or kind words
People not following through on things they said they'd do
Important occasions going unnoticed or gifts feeling careless
Feeling like you're never anyone's priority
Not having enough physical closeness or affection

questions.question: How do you most naturally show someone you love them?
options: Writing them a heartfelt message or telling them directly
Doing something practical to make their life easier
Picking out a gift that perfectly suits them
Spending quality one-on-one time together
Offering a hug, a squeeze or physical reassurance

questions.question: Which memory from a past relationship or friendship means the most to you?
options: When they said something that genuinely moved you
When they stepped in and helped without being asked
A gift that showed they really knew you
A long day spent together doing something you both loved
Falling asleep next to someone who made you feel completely safe

questions.question: You'd feel most disconnected from someone if they:
options: Stopped complimenting you or expressing gratitude
Stopped putting in practical effort for the relationship
Forgot your birthday or never marked special moments
Were always busy and never carved out time for you
Became distant and physically cold or withdrawn

questions.question: On your birthday, the perfect celebration would be:
options: Heartfelt messages, cards and people telling you what you mean to them
Friends rallying together to take things off your plate for the day
A carefully chosen, meaningful gift that shows someone really thought about you
A whole day with the people who matter most, doing things you all love
Lots of hugs, physical celebration and warmth all around you

questions.question: Imagine you're going through something difficult. What helps most?
options: Someone saying exactly the right words at the right time
Someone handling a stressful task you've been dreading
An unexpected thoughtful gesture that shows someone cares
Someone sitting with you and giving you their full attention
Someone holding your hand or sitting physically close to you

questions.question: In a friendship, what do you value most?
options: Friends who regularly check in and tell you they appreciate you
Friends who show up and help when things get hard
Friends who remember what matters to you and mark special moments
Friends who make proper time for you, not just quick catch-ups
Friends who are naturally warm, affectionate and physically present
